label: Economic rate of return benchmarks for ADB project appraisal

err_benchmark.label: Standard minimum economic rate of return (ERR) threshold
err_benchmark.note: ADB traditionally uses 9-12% as the ERR hurdle rate, reflecting the higher opportunity cost of capital in developing Asia.

sensitivity.note: Lower-risk projects in advanced Asian economies (South Korea, Singapore) may use 6%. High-risk projects in LDCs may require 12%.

country_guidance.description: ADB does not mandate a single discount rate. The ERR threshold depends on the borrowing country's opportunity cost of capital.

notes: Like the World Bank, ADB uses ERR as the primary project selection metric. A project is economically viable if ERR > opportunity cost of capital. ADB projects also require a financial internal rate of return (FIRR) analysis alongside the economic analysis.

source.publication: Guidelines for the Economic Analysis of Projects (2017)
